positioning.category: evidence-first behavioral intelligence and automation-of-automation research platform
positioning.primary_user: independent AI systems architect, research engineer, security-conscious automation builder, or technical reviewer
positioning.core_problem: high-risk behavioral or automation analysis often collapses into unverifiable claims, shallow prompts, hidden assumptions, and non-reproducible outputs
positioning.product_promise: convert ambiguous transcript or automation intent into bounded evidence artifacts, questions, gates, contracts, and reviewable reports
explicit_non_claims: not a guilt or intent verdict system
not a fully deployed production SaaS
not a substitute for legal, clinical, employment, or security judgment

product_principles.name: Evidence before persuasion
product_principles.decision_rule: A feature is valuable only if it produces inspectable evidence, a falsifier, or a stronger boundary.

product_principles.name: No unsupported verdicts
product_principles.decision_rule: The product must ask review questions instead of assigning person-level blame, deception, diagnosis, or intent.

product_principles.name: Readable operational state
product_principles.decision_rule: A user must see readiness, gates, limits, unknowns, and release status without reading source code.

product_principles.name: Friction where risk increases
product_principles.decision_rule: Production, public, irreversible, security-sensitive, or person-impacting actions must require explicit gate or human review.

jobs_to_be_done.job: Review a transcript without converting uncertainty into accusation.
jobs_to_be_done.output: verification report with evidence events, hypotheses, uncertainty, missing evidence, and human-review questions
jobs_to_be_done.success_metric: report generated and schema-valid; no forbidden person-level verdict emitted

jobs_to_be_done.job: Convert automation intent into bounded executable contracts.
jobs_to_be_done.output: AOS intent contract, execution contract, score schema, and validation status
jobs_to_be_done.success_metric: AOS kernel validator passes and compile command returns structured contract

jobs_to_be_done.job: Expose product readiness before public GitHub release.
jobs_to_be_done.input: repository state, gates, docs, package metadata
jobs_to_be_done.output: readiness scorecard with pass, unknown, blocked, and human-review items
jobs_to_be_done.success_metric: product readiness endpoint returns non-green state while external CVE and Docker gates are unknown

activation_path.name: Publish candidate only after explicit unknowns are documented
activation_path.evidence: PRODUCT_READINESS_PASS with production_status not GREEN if CVE/Docker are unknown

success_metrics.name: gate_pass_rate
success_metrics.target: all local deterministic gates pass before release candidate

success_metrics.name: readiness_visibility
success_metrics.target: API and UI expose readiness, gates, unknowns, and product stage

success_metrics.name: evidence_completeness
success_metrics.target: critical claims have source, file, test, log, or explicit UNKNOWN

release_policy.allowed_claim: engineering product candidate with local verification evidence
release_policy.forbidden_claim: deployed production system or clinically/legal valid decision engine
release_policy.go_condition: all P0/P1 local gates pass and external CVE/Docker/deployment gates are closed or explicitly waived
release_policy.no_go_condition: missing security audit, broken wheel, failing API smoke, missing human-review boundary, or unsupported person-impacting claim

external_standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF
external_standards_alignment.operational_use: AI risk governance mapped to govern/map/measure/manage style controls

external_standards_alignment.name: NIST SSDF SP 800-218
external_standards_alignment.operational_use: secure software gates, dependency governance, evidence-based release controls

external_standards_alignment.name: OWASP API Security Top 10
external_standards_alignment.operational_use: API auth, input limits, error contract, security headers, threat model coverage

external_standards_alignment.name: CISA Secure by Design
external_standards_alignment.operational_use: secure defaults, explicit customer-facing security posture, no shifted hidden burden
